mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals increasing output and economic momentum, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations when INDPRO is above its 12-month moving average over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment below a critical threshold indicates late-cycle tightness, where labor cost pressures erode margins and forward returns turn negative over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of observations when UNRATE < 4.5% over any 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly low federal funds rates and rising housing starts signal accommodative monetary policy feeding into real estate demand, boosting economic activity and equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The long-short portfolio sorted on joint FEDFUNDS-HOUST z-score fails to produce a statistically significant positive Sharpe ratio (p>0.05) over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above a threshold and is in a tightening trajectory, the discount rate repricing dominates, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when the tightening trigger is active over any 15-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated retail and food services sales reflect strong consumer spending, a key component of GDP, which drives corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The correlation between month-over-month RSAFS growth and subsequent 5-day S&P 500 returns is negative over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Headline CPI inflation rising above a 3% year-over-year threshold indicates overheating that forces future monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of observations when CPI YoY > 3% over any 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicates a robust labor market translating into consumer spending, reinforcing economic expansion and lifting equity prices over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The composite signal from PAYEMS and RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with accuracy above 55% over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rising above a historical threshold signal a recovery in residential investment, a leading indicator of broader economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when HOUST > 1200 over any 15-year period.
